description: Lock book_binding_codex_production to early-medieval codex binding grounded in parchment/vellum surfaces, leather-covered boards, and the older codex form.

checks.reason: The early binding anchor uses parchment/vellum leaves; the broader writing-surface node covers that material substrate better than later paper making.
The St Cuthbert Gospel binding is tooled red leather over boards, making leatherworking a direct material/craft enabler.
Medieval binding elaborates the earlier codex book form rather than inventing bound leaves independently.
Paper making is a later or alternate material path and must not be treated as a direct prerequisite for the early-medieval vellum binding anchor.
